key_insight: The paper shows that bilevel optimization can achieve the optimal second-order stationarity rate with a deterministic single-loop cubic regularized Newton method, even when the upper-level problem is nonconvex.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more efficient and theoretically optimal framework for hyperparameter tuning, meta-learning, and other nested optimization tasks where bilevel structure is common.
method: Builds a double-loop CRN baseline to establish the optimal outer-rate target.
Proposes a single-loop algorithm with one lower-level gradient step plus one Newton step for the hypergradient.
Proves deterministic total oracle complexity of O(epsilon^-1.5) for finding an epsilon-SOSP.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a deterministic O(epsilon^-1.5) total oracle complexity result, matching the optimal SOSP rate known for single-level cubic regularized Newton methods.
watch_item: The convergence guarantee is sensitive to how hypergradient and hyper-Hessian approximations are updated; simple implementation changes may break the proof.
paper_title: On Second-Order Methods for Bilevel Optimization
